name: Alfred Ellis
abstract: Alfred James Rogers (April 21, 1941 – September 23, 2021), known as Pee Wee Ellis due to his diminutive stature, was an American saxophonist, composer, and arranger. With a background in jazz, he was a member of James Brown's band in the 1960s, appearing on many of Brown's recordings and co-writing hits like "Cold Sweat" and "Say It Loud…

about_quotes.speaker: Dick Griffin
about_quotes.evidence: Pee Wee Ellis and I were very good. I was ghost writing for Pee Wee Ellis.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dick Griffin interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, February 12, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Dave Liebman
about_quotes.evidence: That’s how I met Pee Wee Ellis. That’s when I began my relationship with Pee Wee that ended up six years later being the Ellis–Liebman band.
about_quotes.doc_title: Dave Liebman — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Kininger
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with Maceo Parker, Fred Wesley and Pee Wee Ellis, on stage
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Kininger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 27, 2011

about_quotes.speaker: Fred Wesley
about_quotes.evidence: Pee Wee Ellis had made it so that it would be an interesting song... But then when I got to be the band leader... I became 'Fred Wesley and the J.B.'s'
about_quotes.doc_title: Fred Wesley interviewed by Monk Rowe, via Zoom, November 21, 2024

about_quotes.speaker: Sam Kininger
about_quotes.evidence: I got to play with ... Pee Wee Ellis ... Alfred Ellis, what a great guy he is, and he taught us a lot too.
about_quotes.doc_title: Sam Kininger interviewed by Monk Rowe, Clinton, New York, May 27, 2011
